what_it_does: Defines a React InfoPanel component (and a small FeatIcon SVG helper) for the Virtual Hub buyer-facing overlay: a right-side aside showing a property title, four feature badges (2 BED, LIVING, 2 BATH, 1 CAR) rendered as inline SVG icons, a description paragraph, and an 'Enquire about this apartment' CTA. Exposed globally via window.InfoPanel.
observations: FeatIcon helper maps an array of SVG path 'd' strings into <path> elements inside a 24x24 viewBox.
Title falls back to a hardcoded default 'Apartment A — Level 12' when hub?.title is absent.
Four feature badges with hardcoded labels and inline SVG path data for floor/sofa/bath/car glyphs.
Description section uses Lorem ipsum placeholder text and a literal 'Description text' heading.
Close button has aria-label='Close'; uses ✕ glyph.
Component attaches to window.InfoPanel rather than using ES module export.
standouts: Inline SVG path data embedded directly as string arrays for each feature glyph.
Placeholder Lorem ipsum content indicating a mockup/demo stage rather than wired data.
conventions: Follows the kit's vh-* CSS class naming (vh-info-panel, vh-info-title, etc.).
Follows the global-window registration pattern (window.InfoPanel) seen across the kit.
Uses className strings rather than the claude-ds token/registry system; classes carry the styling.
concerns: Hardcoded feature labels and Lorem ipsum body — not yet data-driven from hub.
Only hub.title is consumed; features/description are static regardless of hub prop.
notes: A static-content right-side property info card for the Virtual Hub overlay; demo-grade, mostly hardcoded except the title fallback.
questions: Are the feature badges (beds/baths/car) meant to be driven by hub data, or are they intentionally static for this mockup?
Where do the vh-info-* CSS classes get defined (which stylesheet)?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches the README's description of InfoPanel as the right-side info card (title, features, description, CTA); content is placeholder but structure is as stated.
intention_signals: Aims to present a clean, dismissible property summary card overlaid on an immersive render, with iconography for key apartment attributes and a clear enquire action.
novelty: Inline-array SVG path glyphs for real-estate features (bed/bath/car) as a tiny reusable FeatIcon abstraction.
contribution: Contributes a buyer-facing property info-panel pattern: titled aside with icon feature badges, descriptive body, and a single enquire CTA, plus a compact array-driven SVG icon helper.
summary_for_embedding: React InfoPanel component for the Virtual Hub overlay rendering a property title, icon feature badges, description, and enquire CTA as a right-side aside.
intention_for_embedding: To give buyers a clean overlaid summary card of an apartment with key attributes and a path to enquire.
